title: Skyworks, Qualcomm lead low forward P/E chip stocks as Micron prepares to report
content:
[Close up image of microchip. Integrated circuit IC macro photograph.]
Ismed Syahrul/iStock via Getty Images

Micron Technology (MU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MU]) is set to report quarterly results after Wednesday’s closing bell, with investors looking for another strong print from the memory chipmaker as demand tied to artificial intelligence infrastructure continues to expand.

Against this backdrop of AI‑driven enthusiasm for semiconductors, below is a list of the top 10 chip related stocks above $2B market cap, ranked by their lowest forward price‑to‑earnings ratios, along with their valuation grades.

The list is topped by Skyworks Solutions (SWKS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SWKS]), with a ratio of 11.49. QUALCOMM (QCOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QCOM]) and Qorvo (QRVO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QRVO]) follow closely behind. Micron Technology (MU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MU]) and Cirrus Logic (CRUS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRUS]) round out the top five.

The list includes a range of market capitalizations, from smaller players like Photronics (PLAB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PLAB]) at $2.04B to industry giants like Micron Technology at $519.64B and NVIDIA at $4.42T. While NVIDIA (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA]) appears on the list, its forward P/E ratio of 22.05 is notably higher than the leaders at the top.

P/E FWD (forward price-to-earnings ratio) is a valuation multiple that compares a stock’s current share price to expected (forward-looking) earnings per share, typically based on analysts’ consensus estimates for the upcoming year.

Here is the list:

 	*
Skyworks Solutions (SWKS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SWKS]), P/E fwd ratio: 11.49, Valuation grade: A

 	*
QUALCOMM (QCOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QCOM]), P/E fwd ratio: 11.83, Valuation grade: B-

 	*
Qorvo (QRVO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QRVO]), P/E fwd ratio: 11.92, Valuation grade: B+

 	*
Micron Technology (MU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MU]), P/E fwd ratio: 12.65, Valuation grade: B-

 	*
Cirrus Logic (CRUS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRUS]), P/E fwd ratio: 15.01, Valuation grade: B-

 	*
Photronics (PLAB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PLAB]), P/E fwd ratio: 15.98, Valuation grade: A

 	*
Synaptics (SYNA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SYNA]), P/E fwd ratio: 16.82, Valuation grade: C+

 	*
Universal Display (OLED [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OLED]), P/E fwd ratio: 19.50, Valuation grade: D+

 	*
ON Semiconductor (ON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ON]), P/E fwd ratio: 20.93, Valuation grade: B-

 	*
NVIDIA (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA]), P/E fwd ratio: 22.05, Valuation grade: F

Semiconductor ETFs: (SMH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SMH]), (SOXX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SOXX]), (SOXL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SOXL]), (FTXL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FTXL]), (XSD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XSD]), (USD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/USD]), (PSI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PSI]), and (SEMI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SEMI])
